title: Choosing Your Path: University or Vocational School?
passage:
Choosing Your Path: University or Vocational School?

After finishing high school, young people must choose what to do next. Many students choose higher education. Higher education means going to a university or college. At university, students study academic subjects like business, history, science, or medicine. They spend three to four years in classrooms and libraries. They read many books and write long essays. After they finish their studies, they receive a university degree. This degree helps them get professional jobs in offices, schools, or hospitals.

However, other students choose vocational training. Vocational training is different. It is also called trade school or professional training. At a vocational school, students do not study general academic subjects. Instead, they learn practical skills for a specific job. For example, they can learn to become a car mechanic, a cook, an electrician, or a plumber. These courses are usually short. They last from six months to two years. Students do not write many essays. Instead, they practice with their hands in workshops.

Both choices have good points. University graduates often earn more money in the future. They also have many different job choices. However, university is very expensive, and students often have to pay a lot of money. It also takes a long time to finish.

On the other hand, vocational school is cheaper and faster. Students can start working and earning money quickly. Also, society always needs practical workers like builders, electricians, and plumbers. In conclusion, both paths are good. Students should choose university if they like studying books. They should choose vocational training if they like working with their hands.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Students at university study academic subjects like business or science.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'At university, students study academic subjects like business, history, science, or medicine.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: University courses usually last for six months.
questions.explanation: The passage states that university students 'spend three to four years', while vocational courses last 'from six months to two years'.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Vocational training is also called trade school.
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Vocational training is different. It is also called trade school or professional training.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Most vocational school students want to work in hospitals.
questions.explanation: The passage mentions hospitals as a workplace for university graduates, but it does not mention where most vocational school students want to work.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do university students write during their studies?
questions.explanation: The text directly states: 'They read many books and write long essays.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How long do vocational training courses usually last?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'These courses are usually short. They last from six months to two years.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Which job is mentioned as an example of vocational training?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'For example, they can learn to become a car mechanic, a cook, an electrician, or a plumber.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why do some people choose NOT to go to university?

options.A: Because they do not like classrooms.
options.B: Because it is expensive and takes a long time.
options.C: Because they want to write essays.
options.D: Because they want to study history.

questions.explanation: The passage says: 'However, university is very expensive... It also takes a long time to finish.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: After finishing university, students receive a university ______.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'After they finish their studies, they receive a university degree.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Vocational students practice with their hands in ______.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Instead, they practice with their hands in workshops.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Compared to university, vocational school is ______ and faster.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'On the other hand, vocational school is cheaper and faster.'

vocabulary.meaning: education at a college or university
training that teaches you the skills you need to do a particular job
related to subjects that involve thinking and studying, not practical skills
relating to experience, real situations, or actions rather than ideas or imagination
a room or building where things are made or repaired using tools

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài đạt điểm cao (A1-A2)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Ở trình độ này, câu hỏi sử dụng các từ giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy tìm các từ khóa nổi bật như 'six months', 'degree', hoặc 'workshops' để định vị thông tin nhanh chóng.

2. True/False/Not Given: Nếu bạn hoàn toàn không tìm thấy thông tin nào nói về ý của câu hỏi (ví dụ: học sinh trường nghề muốn làm việc ở bệnh viện), hãy chọn ngay NOT GIVEN thay vì đoán mò.

3. Điền từ vào chỗ trống: Hãy chú ý giới hạn từ (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Từ bạn điền phải được lấy trực tiếp từ bài đọc, không cần thay đổi dạng của từ (không thêm -s, -ed nếu trong bài không có).
